title: abies forrestii subsp. chayuensis
intro: Abies forrestii subsp. chayuensis, also known as the Chayu silver fir, is a subspecies of Abies forrestii, a species of evergreen fir native to the mountainous regions of the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau in China. This plant has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Abies forrestii subsp. chayuensis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's essential oils and resins are often extracted and used to create medicinal teas, infusions, and ointments.
